name: Artichoke
description: Artichokes are large, thistle-like perennials best grown in mild climates with long, cool growing seasons for optimal bud development. Plant in full sun and well-drained soil, ensuring ample space and consistent moisture for healthy growth.
notes: Protect crowns from hard freezes to maintain perennial status in colder zones.

name: Cardoon
description: Cardoons are a striking perennial vegetable grown for their edible, blanched leaf stalks. Plant in full sun with fertile, well-drained soil and consistent moisture for best growth.
notes: Blanch stalks for 3-4 weeks before harvest to tenderize and reduce bitterness.

name: Chinese Cabbage
description: Chinese Cabbage, a napa type, thrives in cool weather and can bolt quickly in heat. Ensure consistent moisture and fertile soil for dense, mild-flavored heads.
notes: Protect from flea beetles and ensure rapid growth for best head quality.

name: Husk Cherry
description: Husk cherries are vigorous, sprawling nightshades that produce small, sweet-tart fruits encased in a papery husk. They thrive in warm conditions and are harvested when fruits drop to the ground, indicating ripeness.
notes: Monitor for pests like flea beetles and provide ample space for sprawling growth.

name: Shoots
description: Shoots are rapidly growing young plants, typically harvested within 1-2 weeks of seeding for their tender stems and leaves. They are ideal for dense, shallow plantings and can be grown year-round indoors or in mild outdoor conditions.
notes: Ensure consistent moisture and good air circulation to prevent damping off.

name: Kalettes
description: Kalettes thrive in cool weather and rich, well-drained soil, producing mini kale-like sprouts along a central stalk. Harvest continuously from the bottom up as florets mature, often after a light frost for improved flavor.
notes: A light frost can enhance their sweet, nutty flavor.

name: Microgreens
description: Microgreens are fast-growing seedlings harvested at their cotyledon or first true leaf stage. They are typically direct-sown densely in trays or beds and ready for harvest in just 1-3 weeks.
notes: Ensure good air circulation to prevent damping off, and maintain consistent shallow moisture.

name: Broccoli Raab
description: A fast-growing Brassica, Broccoli Raab thrives in cool weather, producing tender leaves, stalks, and small florets. Harvest promptly when flower buds begin to form to prevent bolting and maintain tenderness.
notes: Rapidly bolts in warm temperatures; successive plantings every 2-3 weeks are ideal for continuous harvest.

name: Belgian Endive
description: Grow roots in the field from direct-sown seed during summer, aiming for approximately 4-6 inches in diameter by late fall. After harvesting and curing roots, force them in a dark, cool, humid environment for 3-4 weeks to produce crisp, bitter-free chicons.
notes: Ensure deep, loose soil for optimal root development before forcing to yield high-quality chicons.

name: Frisée
description: Frisée is a cool-season chicory known for its curly, slightly bitter leaves. Blanching the heart 7-10 days before harvest will yield a more tender and less bitter product.
notes: Provide consistent moisture to prevent bitterness and bolting in warmer temperatures.

name: Dandelion Greens
description: Dandelion Greens are hardy, cool-season herbs known for their slightly bitter, nutritious leaves. They can be direct seeded or transplanted and prefer well-drained soil.
notes: Harvest young leaves frequently to encourage new growth and reduce bitterness, ensuring consistent moisture.

name: Chard
description: Chard is a versatile leafy green, thriving in cool temperatures but tolerating some heat. Harvest outer leaves regularly to encourage continuous production throughout the season.
notes: Ensure consistent moisture to prevent bolting and maintain tender leaves, especially during warmer spells.

name: Artichoke Jerusalem
description: Jerusalem Artichokes, also known as sunchokes, produce edible knobby tubers that can be harvested in late fall after frost. This hardy perennial is easy to grow and can become invasive if not managed.
notes: Harvest tubers after frost for best flavor and sweetness, or as needed throughout winter in mild climates.

name: Yacon
description: Yacon is a perennial plant grown for its crunchy, sweet, water-rich tubers, resembling jicama or crisp apple. It requires a long, warm growing season and is typically harvested after the first frost or when foliage dies back.
notes: Plant rhizomes in well-drained, fertile soil after the last frost; tubers develop best in full sun and consistent moisture.

name: Mashua
description: Mashua is an Andean tuber crop related to nasturtiums, prized for its edible peppery tubers. It thrives in cool climates, producing a substantial yield after a long growing season.
notes: Ensure consistent moisture and harvest after foliage dies back for best tuber development.

name: Ulluco
description: Ulluco is a colorful Andean tuber crop known for its vibrant red, yellow, and purple tubers and high nutritional value. It thrives in cool, moist climates with a long growing season, preferring well-drained soil and regular irrigation, but is sensitive to heat and hard frost.
notes: Ensure consistent moisture and good hilling; harvest tubers before the first hard frost.

name: Agrostemma
description: Agrostemma is a charming cool-season annual known for its delicate, tall pink flowers that are excellent for cutting. It prefers full sun and well-drained soil, blooming profusely from late spring to early summer.
notes: Supports pollinators and can reseed readily.

name: Ammobium
description: Ammobium is a unique everlasting flower, known for its papery white blooms ideal for drying. Grow in full sun and well-drained soil, ensuring consistent moisture for best stem production.
notes: Harvest flowers when fully open but before centers turn yellow, drying upside down in a dark, well-ventilated area.

name: Ammi
description: Ammi thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, preferring cooler temperatures for establishment. Direct sow seeds in succession in spring for continuous blooms, ensuring regular moisture until plants are established.
notes: Harvest stems when flowers are partially open for best vase life.

name: Asclepias
description: Asclepias, commonly known as milkweed, is a vital perennial host plant for monarch butterflies. It produces clusters of orange, pink, or white flowers throughout summer, providing nectar for various pollinators and essential food for monarch larvae.
notes: Ensure full sun and well-drained soil for best growth and monarch attraction.

name: Atriplex
description: Atriplex, known for its vibrant red-purple foliage, is a striking ornamental annual that adds color and texture to garden beds and containers. Thriving in cool weather and full sun, it is relatively easy to grow from seed, providing continuous visual interest throughout the season until frost.
notes: Pinch young plants for bushier growth and richer foliage color.

name: Bells of Ireland
description: Bells of Ireland are grown for their striking green, bell-shaped calyxes that encase tiny white flowers, making them a unique and popular choice for cut flower arrangements. They prefer cool growing conditions, well-drained soil, and should be transplanted after the last frost for best results.
notes: Ensure consistent moisture and protect from extreme heat to prevent bolting and ensure strong stem development.

name: Bupleurum
description: Bupleurum is a delicate annual known for its wispy, chartreuse foliage and small, star-shaped flowers, making it an excellent filler for bouquets. It thrives in full sun to partial shade and prefers well-drained soil, providing abundant cuts from summer to fall.
notes: For optimal vase life, harvest stems when the flowers are fully open but before seeds begin to set.

name: Carthamus
description: Safflower (Carthamus tinctorius) is a warm-season annual grown for its striking orange, thistle-like blooms. It thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, making it an excellent choice for cut flower arrangements.
notes: Harvest when flowers are fully open but before they start to dry out for the best vase life. Wear gloves due to thistles.

name: Centaurea
description: Centaurea, also known as Bachelor's Button, is a hardy annual known for its vibrant blue, pink, or white blooms. It prefers full sun and well-drained soil, blooming profusely from late spring into summer if deadheaded regularly.
notes: Deadhead spent blooms to encourage continuous flowering and prevent excessive self-seeding.

name: Cerinthe
description: Cerinthe, also known as honeywort, is an attractive annual flower prized for its blue-green foliage and drooping blue bracts that attract pollinators. It thrives in cool weather and can be direct sown in early spring for a summer-long display.
notes: Deadhead spent blooms to encourage continuous flowering and collect seeds for self-sowing.

name: Clarkia
description: Clarkia, also known as Farewell-to-Spring, thrives in full sun to partial shade and well-drained soil. This elegant annual produces delicate, ruffled blooms, perfect for cutting or adding a cottage garden charm.
notes: Pinching young plants encourages bushier growth and a longer flowering period.

name: Craspedia
description: Craspedia thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, preferring moderate fertility. Harvest stems when flowers are fully colored and firm for both fresh use and drying.
notes: Excellent for drying; harvest at full maturity for best results and hang upside down in a cool, dark place.

name: Cynoglossum
description: Cynoglossum, also known as Chinese Forget-Me-Not, produces delicate blue flowers ideal for cut flower arrangements or garden borders. It is an easy-to-grow annual that benefits from full sun and well-drained soil.
notes: Deadhead regularly to encourage continuous blooming and extend the harvest window.

name: Daucus
description: Daucus (Queen Anne's Lace) thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, often self-seeding in ideal conditions. For cut flower production, direct sow seeds in spring or fall and harvest when the umbels are fully open but before shedding pollen.
notes: Deadhead spent blooms for continuous cutting, or allow some to go to seed for self-seeding.

name: Didiscus
description: Delicate annual flowers known for their charming blue, white, or pink lace-like blooms. They thrive in mild conditions and are excellent for bouquets.
notes: Requires consistent moisture and protection from strong winds.

name: Digitalis
description: Digitalis, also known as foxglove, produces dramatic tall spikes of bell-shaped flowers. As a biennial, it typically establishes foliage in its first year and flowers profusely in its second year.
notes: Plant young seedlings in fall or early spring for blooms in the following growing season. Provide staking for taller varieties.

name: Dianthus
description: Dianthus thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, preferring cooler temperatures. Plant as transplants in spring or fall for continuous blooms, deadheading regularly to encourage new growth.
notes: Ensure good air circulation to prevent fungal diseases.

name: Eryngium
description: Eryngium, or sea holly, produces striking, architectural blue metallic flowers. It is a drought-tolerant plant valued for its unique texture and long vase life in floral arrangements.
notes: Requires excellent drainage and full sun. Best started from transplants for timely harvest.

name: Eucalyptus
description: Eucalyptus thrives in full sun and well-draining soil, needing consistent moisture until established. Harvest stems for their aromatic foliage once they reach a desirable length, often allowing for multiple cuts from mature plants.
notes: Prune regularly to encourage bushier growth and abundant foliage production.

name: Gomphrena
description: Gomphrena, also known as Globe Amaranth, produces vibrant, clover-like flower heads ideal for cut flowers and everlasting arrangements. It is a heat-loving annual that thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, blooming continuously from summer until the first frost.
notes: Excellent for dried floral arrangements due to its papery texture and long-lasting color.

name: Gypsophila
description: Gypsophila thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, producing a profusion of tiny white flowers on airy stems. Transplant seedlings after the last frost for best results and continuous harvesting over several weeks.
notes: Pinch young plants for bushier growth and higher stem count.

name: Helipterum
description: Helipterum is a charming annual paper daisy, known for its vibrant, papery blooms that are excellent for cutting and drying. Plant in full sun in well-draining soil after all danger of frost has passed for a continuous display of pink and white flowers.
notes: Harvest flowers just as they open fully for the best drying quality.

name: Lupine
description: Lupines are stunning perennial flowers known for their tall, colorful spires of densely packed pea-like blooms, perfect for adding vertical interest to garden beds and bouquets. They thrive in full sun to partial shade and well-draining, slightly acidic soil, preferring cooler climates and often flowering in early summer.
notes: Deadhead spent blooms to encourage reblooming and maintain plant vigor.

name: Matricaria
description: Matricaria, also known as feverfew, is an easy-to-grow annual known for its small, prolific white daisy-like flowers. Plant in full sun and well-draining soil, ensuring consistent moisture for continuous blooms throughout the season.
notes: Deadhead regularly to encourage more blooms; plants may self-seed for next year's crop.

name: Monarda
description: Monarda, or Bee Balm, is a vibrant perennial known for attracting pollinators. It thrives in full sun to partial shade with good air circulation to prevent powdery mildew.
notes: Divide every 3-4 years to maintain vigor and increase yields; deadhead for prolonged blooming.

name: Nicotiana
description: Nicotiana, or flowering tobacco, produces fragrant blooms, especially in the evening, adding beauty and scent to the garden. It thrives in full sun to partial shade and prefers rich, well-draining soil.
notes: Deadhead spent blooms regularly to encourage continuous flowering throughout the season. Provide consistent moisture for best performance.

name: Orlaya
description: Orlaya grandiflora, also known as White Lace Flower, produces delicate white umbels resembling lace. It's a beautiful filler or standalone flower for bouquets, valued for its airy texture and long vase life if harvested at the correct stage.
notes: Harvest when flower heads are mostly open but before pollen sheds for best vase life.

name: Phacelia
description: Phacelia is a fast-growing annual known for its vibrant blue-purple flowers and excellent bee attraction. It thrives in cool weather and can be direct-sown in early spring or fall as a beneficial insectary plant or cover crop.
notes: Attracts a wide range of beneficial insects and pollinators; excellent for improving soil health and suppressing weeds.

name: Poppy
description: Poppies thrive when direct sown in early spring in well-drained soil. They prefer full sun and cooler temperatures, blooming quickly before the intense summer heat.
notes: Sow seeds on the soil surface as they require light to germinate, then thin seedlings for optimal growth.

name: Portulaca
description: Portulaca, also known as Moss Rose, is a heat-loving annual known for its vibrant, succulent blooms and drought tolerance. It thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, making it excellent for borders, containers, and rock gardens.
notes: Ensure good drainage to prevent root rot; flowers close on cloudy days and at night.

name: Saponaria
description: Saponaria, also known as Soapwort, is a charming annual flower perfect for rock gardens or borders. It produces abundant clusters of small pink or white flowers and is known for its low-maintenance growth and ability to attract pollinators.
notes: Deadhead spent blooms to encourage continuous flowering throughout the season.

name: Stock
description: Stock thrives in cool weather and well-drained soil, producing fragrant, upright flower spikes. Pinching is generally not recommended for single-stem production, aiming for one strong central spike per plant.
notes: Provide support (netting) for straight stems, especially for tall varieties.

name: Tithonia
description: Tithonia is a striking annual flower that thrives in full sun and hot weather, producing a continuous display of bright orange, daisy-like blooms. It requires well-drained soil and ample space to reach its impressive height, making it an excellent backdrop or specimen plant.
notes: Deadhead regularly to encourage continuous blooming; provide support for tall varieties in windy areas.

name: Verbena
description: Verbena thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, providing continuous blooms from late spring until frost. Regular deadheading encourages new growth and extends the flowering season.
notes: Pinch young plants to encourage bushier growth and more abundant blooms.

name: Xeranthemum
description: Xeranthemum, known as immortelle, is an easy-to-grow annual treasured for its papery, everlasting blooms. It thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, producing abundant flowers perfect for drying.
notes: Harvest flowers when fully open but before seeds set, then hang upside down in a dark, dry place for best drying results.

name: Cress Ornamental
description: Cress Ornamental is a fast-growing annual appreciated for its delicate, lacy foliage and quick ground cover. Ideal for cool season plantings, it adds fine texture and a vibrant green pop to garden beds and containers before warm weather causes it to bolt.
notes: Ensure consistent moisture for best foliage development and sow successional plantings for continuous display.

name: Tulip
description: Plant tulip bulbs in fall in well-drained soil, ensuring they receive a proper cold dormancy period for vibrant spring blooms. Harvest cut flowers when buds show color but are still closed, allowing them to open fully in the vase for extended enjoyment.
notes: Ensure excellent drainage to prevent bulb rot and protect bulbs from rodents like voles and squirrels.

name: Crocus
description: Crocus are early-blooming spring bulbs that provide vibrant color at the tail end of winter. They are very cold hardy and naturalize well, creating beautiful drifts over time.
notes: Plant bulbs in fall, 3-4 inches deep, in well-drained soil for best spring blooms.

name: Morning Glory
description: Start seeds indoors or direct sow after all danger of frost has passed, providing a sturdy trellis for their vigorous twining habit. Ensure full sun exposure and well-drained soil to encourage continuous blooming throughout the warm season.
notes: Soak seeds overnight before planting to improve germination rates.

name: Phlox
description: Annual phlox (Phlox drummondii) produces vibrant, fragrant clusters of blooms ideal for borders and cutting gardens. Thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, blooming from early summer until frost.
notes: Pinch young plants to encourage bushier growth and more abundant blooms.

name: Columbine
description: Columbine is a hardy perennial known for its unique spurred flowers in spring to early summer. It thrives in partial shade and well-drained soil, attracting pollinators to the garden.
notes: Deer resistant and low maintenance, ideal for cottage gardens and naturalized areas.

name: Cornflower
description: Cornflowers are easy-to-grow annuals prized for their vibrant blue, pink, or white blooms. They prefer full sun and well-drained soil, blooming best in cool weather and benefiting from successive plantings for continuous harvest.
notes: Deadhead regularly for continuous blooms; flowers are excellent for cutting and drying.

name: Bee Balm
description: A vibrant perennial known for its unique tubular flowers that attract bees, hummingbirds, and butterflies. Thrives in full sun to partial shade with moist, well-drained soil, blooming from mid-summer.
notes: Deadhead spent blooms to encourage more flowering and prevent excessive self-seeding.

name: Hollyhock
description: A classic cottage garden biennial, Hollyhocks thrive in full sun and well-drained soil. Sow seeds in late spring or early summer for blooms the following year, ensuring adequate air circulation to prevent rust.
notes: Provide staking for tall varieties and monitor for rust disease, especially in humid conditions.

name: Ageratum
description: Ageratum, also known as Floss Flower, produces fuzzy blue clusters ideal for cut flower arrangements or bedding. It thrives in full sun with consistent moisture and good air circulation, blooming continuously until frost.
notes: Pinch young plants to encourage bushier growth and more blooms.

name: Alyssum
description: Alyssum is a low-growing, spreading annual flower known for its abundant clusters of tiny, fragrant blossoms that form a carpet of white, pink, or purple. It thrives in full sun to partial shade and is excellent for groundcover, rock gardens, and container edges.
notes: Trim back after the first flush of blooms to encourage reblooming throughout the season, and keep soil consistently moist.

name: Angelica
description: Angelica is a striking architectural biennial herb, preferring cool, moist conditions and rich soil. Harvest young leaves and stems in its first year for culinary use, or allow it to overwinter for seeds and roots in the second year.
notes: Ensure consistent moisture and partial shade in hotter climates; avoid waterlogging.

name: Anise Hyssop
description: Anise Hyssop is a beautiful perennial herb with licorice-scented leaves and spikes of purple flowers, highly attractive to bees and other pollinators. It thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, offering a continuous harvest of leaves and flowers throughout the growing season.
notes: Cut back periodically to encourage new growth and prolong harvest of both leaves and flowers.

name: Ginseng
description: Ginseng is a slow-growing perennial herb valued for its medicinal root. It requires deep shade and several years to reach maturity before the roots are harvested in the fall.
notes: Requires well-drained, rich soil in a deeply shaded location, often under deciduous trees or artificial shade.

name: Goldenseal
description: A shade-loving perennial medicinal herb native to eastern North America, Goldenseal is valued for its rhizomes used in traditional medicine. It requires several years to mature, thriving in rich, moist, well-drained forest soils.
notes: Thrives in deep shade and rich, organic forest soils; protect from full sun.

name: Hyssop
description: Hyssop is a hardy perennial herb that thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, tolerating drought once established. It produces aromatic leaves and blue-purple flowers, ideal for culinary, medicinal, or ornamental use.
notes: Regular harvesting of leaves and flowers encourages bushier growth and prevents plants from becoming too woody.

name: Mountain Mint
description: Mountain Mint is a hardy native perennial herb, highly attractive to pollinators and relatively low-maintenance once established. It prefers full sun to partial shade and well-drained soil, spreading vigorously over time.
notes: Vigorous spreader, consider containment or ample space. Excellent for attracting a wide variety of native pollinators.

name: Rue
description: Rue is a resilient perennial herb known for its striking blue-green foliage and strong, bitter aroma. Plant in full sun and well-drained soil, allowing ample space for its bushy growth habit to develop.
notes: Handle with care as sap can cause skin irritation; wear gloves when harvesting.

name: Saffron Crocus
description: Saffron Crocus is cultivated for its valuable red stigmas, harvested from the purple flowers. Plant corms in late summer for fall flowering and hand-harvest stigmas daily as flowers open.
notes: Ensure excellent drainage to prevent corm rot; harvest stigmas immediately after flowers open for best quality.

name: Salad Burnet
description: Salad Burnet is a charming perennial herb known for its cucumber-flavored, serrated leaves, ideal for salads and garnishes. It's a low-maintenance plant that thrives in full sun to partial shade and well-drained soil, providing continuous harvest through the growing season.
notes: Harvest outer leaves regularly to encourage new growth and prevent bolting.

name: Saltwort
description: Saltwort thrives in sandy, well-drained soil and tolerates saline conditions, making it suitable for coastal or brackish environments. Harvest the succulent, crisp shoots when young for a delicate, slightly salty flavor.
notes: Consider amending soil with a small amount of sea salt for enhanced flavor.

name: Savory
description: Summer savory thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, producing aromatic leaves ideal for culinary use. It can be direct sown after the last frost or started indoors and transplanted, requiring consistent moisture but not overly wet conditions.
notes: Pinch back regularly to encourage bushier growth and prevent premature flowering.

name: Shiso
description: Shiso thrives in warm weather and full sun, preferring well-drained, fertile soil. Regular harvesting of leaves encourages bushy growth and extends the production season.
notes: Pinch back flower stalks to prolong leaf production and prevent premature bolting.

name: Valerian
description: Tall medicinal herb known for its calming properties, producing white to pinkish flowers. It is primarily harvested for its root in the second year of growth.
notes: Harvest roots in late fall of the second year for optimal potency. Requires well-drained soil.

name: Catnip
description: Catnip is a vigorous perennial herb known for its attractant qualities to felines. It thrives in full sun to partial shade and well-drained soil, establishing easily and producing multiple harvests throughout the growing season.
notes: Regular harvesting encourages new growth and bushier plants.

name: Mexican Mint Marigold
description: Mexican Mint Marigold thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, producing anise-flavored leaves. It's an excellent tarragon substitute and can be harvested continuously throughout the warm season.
notes: Pinch regularly to encourage bushiness and continuous leaf production.

name: Astragalus
description: Astragalus is a hardy perennial legume grown primarily for its immune-boosting roots. It requires patience as roots are typically harvested after 2-4 years of growth, thriving in well-drained soil and full sun.
notes: Ensure well-drained soil to prevent root rot, especially during winter dormancy; harvest roots in fall once mature.

name: Marshmallow Root
description: Marshmallow Root (Althaea officinalis) is a hardy perennial herb cultivated for its demulcent roots. It prefers full sun to partial shade, moist well-drained soil, and is typically harvested in the fall of its second year for optimal root development.
notes: Harvest roots in autumn after the first or second growing season for best quality.

name: Motherwort
description: Motherwort is a robust perennial herb that thrives in full sun to partial shade and prefers well-drained, fertile soil. Harvest the aerial parts, including leaves and flowering tops, for medicinal use typically from its second year onwards or late in its first growing season.
notes: Harvest aerial parts before or during flowering for best medicinal quality.

name: Skullcap
description: Skullcap (Scutellaria lateriflora) is a calming perennial herb with small, hooded blue flowers. It prefers moist, rich soil and partial shade, making it well-suited for understory planting or shaded garden beds.
notes: Ensure consistent moisture and consider mulching to maintain soil humidity, especially in warmer climates.

name: Comfrey
description: Comfrey is a vigorous perennial herb known for its deep taproot and dynamic nutrient accumulation. It's primarily grown for chop-and-drop mulch, compost tea, or medicinal uses, producing abundant biomass.
notes: Requires deep, fertile soil and consistent moisture for best growth; avoid disturbing established plants.

name: Wormwood
description: Wormwood is a hardy perennial herb known for its bitter medicinal properties and silvery foliage. It thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, making it relatively easy to grow and maintain in temperate climates.
notes: Ensure good air circulation to prevent fungal issues and harvest before flowering for best medicinal potency.

name: Elecampane
description: A tall, stately perennial herb known for its large, medicinal roots. It thrives in full sun to partial shade and prefers rich, well-drained soil, typically harvested in its second or third year.
notes: Ensure deep, loose soil for optimal root development and good drainage to prevent rot.

name: Milk Thistle
description: Milk Thistle is a hardy annual herb grown for its medicinal seeds, primarily used as a liver tonic. It prefers full sun and well-drained soil, maturing in about 100-120 days from planting.
notes: Harvest seeds carefully as they shatter easily once ripe.

name: Passionflower
description: Passionflower is a vigorous perennial vine that thrives in full sun with consistent moisture. Provide strong trellising or support for its climbing habit, harvesting leaves and flowers throughout the warm season.
notes: Ensure sturdy vertical support is in place before planting; benefits from mulching to retain soil moisture.

name: Tulsi
description: Tulsi thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, requiring consistent warmth and protection from frost. Pinch back flower buds regularly to encourage bushy growth and extend leaf production for continuous harvests.
notes: Ensure good air circulation to prevent fungal diseases.

name: Blackberry
description: Blackberries are perennial bramble fruits known for their sweet, dark berries. They require full sun, well-drained soil, and trellising for optimal support and yield.
notes: Prune primocanes and floricanes annually for best production and disease management.

name: Blueberry
description: Blueberries thrive in full sun and well-drained, highly acidic soil with a pH of 4.5-5.5. They require consistent moisture and sufficient chilling hours during winter for optimal fruit production.
notes: Maintain soil pH with peat moss or sulfur; protect from birds.

name: Gooseberry
description: Hardy, deciduous berry shrubs thriving in cool climates with well-drained soil. Requires annual pruning for best fruit production and disease prevention.
notes: Ensure good air circulation to prevent powdery mildew, and protect ripening fruit from birds.

name: Jostaberry
description: Jostaberries are hardy, vigorous perennial shrubs that produce tart, dark berries. Plant in full sun to partial shade in well-drained soil, and prune annually for best fruit production.
notes: Ensure good air circulation and regular pruning to prevent fungal diseases.

name: Sea Buckthorn
description: A hardy, deciduous shrub known for its tart, orange berries. It is a nitrogen fixer, thriving in poor soils and producing nutritious fruit in late summer to fall.
notes: Requires both male and female plants for fruit production (typically 1 male per 8-10 females).

name: Currant
description: Currants are hardy, low-maintenance perennial shrubs that thrive in cool climates and well-drained soil. They produce small, tart berries in clusters, ripening from early to mid-summer.
notes: Ensure good air circulation and prune annually to encourage vigorous growth and maximize fruit production.

name: Fig
description: Figs are deciduous trees producing sweet, soft fruit in warm climates. They require full sun, well-drained soil, and protection from severe frosts to thrive and produce abundant harvests.
notes: Ensure good drainage and protect young trees from winter cold.

name: Persimmon
description: Persimmons are deciduous trees that require a long, warm growing season for optimal fruit development. Harvest fruit when fully colored, noting that astringent types must be bletted or fully soft before consumption.
notes: Ensure adequate drainage and full sun exposure for best fruit production. Consider both astringent (e.g., Hachiya) and non-astringent (e.g., Fuyu) varieties.

name: Pawpaw
description: Pawpaws are understory trees native to eastern North America, preferring rich, moist soils and partial shade when young, though full sun is tolerated by mature trees. They require cross-pollination from another genetically distinct pawpaw tree for fruit production, thriving in USDA zones 5-9.
notes: Ensure good drainage and plant at least two different genetic varieties for cross-pollination.

name: Mulberry
description: Mulberry trees produce abundant sweet berries in various colors (black, white, red) over an extended season. They are vigorous growers, hardy, and require minimal care once established, providing excellent fruit for fresh eating, preserves, and drying.
notes: Ensure adequate space for mature tree size. Fruit is highly perishable and stains easily.

name: Quince
description: Quince is a hardy deciduous tree valued for its aromatic pome fruits, which are typically too hard to eat raw and are best cooked into jams, jellies, and desserts. It prefers full sun, well-drained soil, and requires a chilling period to fruit effectively.
notes: A perennial tree crop; DTM reflects days from planting a young tree (sapling) to first significant harvest, typically 3-5 years. Spacing is for trees within an orchard row.

name: Medlar
description: Medlar trees thrive in temperate climates, requiring a cold winter for good fruit set. Fruits are harvested in late autumn/early winter, typically after a few frosts, and must be 'bletted' (softened) before consumption.
notes: Harvest after first hard frosts and allow fruit to blet for optimal flavor.

name: Hardy Kiwi
description: Hardy Kiwi are vigorous, perennial vining fruits that require a sturdy trellis system for support. They need both male and female plants for fruit production, and take 3-5 years to begin bearing fruit.
notes: Ensure proper male-to-female plant ratio (1 male per 8-10 females) and strong trellis support.

name: Honeyberry
description: Honeyberries are hardy, early-fruiting shrubs, offering the first berries of spring. They are exceptionally cold-tolerant and require minimal maintenance once established.
notes: Requires at least two different varieties for optimal cross-pollination and fruit set.

name: Lingonberry
description: Low-growing, evergreen shrubs native to northern regions, producing tart, red berries. Thrives in acidic, well-drained soil and partial shade, requiring several years to establish for full production.
notes: Requires highly acidic soil (pH 4.5-5.5) and consistent moisture. Benefits from organic mulch like pine needles or sawdust.

name: Huckleberry
description: Huckleberries are deciduous shrubs native to North America, closely related to blueberries. They produce small, flavorful berries, often purplish-black, valued for their sweet-tart taste and antioxidant properties.
notes: Requires acidic soil (pH 4.5-5.5) and excellent drainage for best establishment and fruit production.

name: Serviceberry
description: Hardy deciduous shrub or small tree producing small, sweet, edible berries in early summer. Thrives in full sun to partial shade and well-drained soil, offering both ornamental beauty and a nutritious fruit harvest.
notes: Prune annually to maintain health, shape, and fruit production; protect from bird predation as berries ripen.

name: Nanking Cherry
description: Nanking cherry is a resilient fruiting shrub known for its small, tart cherries that ripen in early summer. It is extremely cold hardy, drought tolerant, and provides an abundant harvest suitable for fresh eating, jams, and jellies.
notes: Requires cross-pollination for best fruit set, so plant at least two shrubs.

name: Beach Plum
description: Beach Plums are hardy, native shrubs thriving in coastal conditions, producing tart, edible plums in late summer. Ideal for jams and jellies, they require minimal care once established and benefit from cross-pollination for better fruit set.
notes: Requires full sun and well-drained, sandy soil; benefits from cross-pollination for better fruit set.

name: Autumn Olive
description: A hardy, nitrogen-fixing shrub known for its abundant tart, edible berries in late summer to fall. It is highly adaptable to various soil conditions and climates, requiring minimal care once established.
notes: Extremely vigorous and adaptable, Autumn Olive can become invasive in some regions; consider local regulations and choose non-invasive varieties if available.

name: Juneberry
description: Juneberries are hardy, low-maintenance shrubs or small trees, producing sweet, blueberry-like fruits in early summer. They thrive in full sun to partial shade and a wide range of well-drained soils.
notes: Ensure good drainage and irrigate during dry periods for optimal fruit production; protect early blooms from late frosts.

name: Cornelian Cherry
description: A resilient shrub or small tree, Cornelian Cherries are prized for their tart, ruby-red fruits that ripen in late summer. They thrive in full sun to partial shade and well-drained soil, requiring minimal care once established.
notes: Requires cross-pollination for best fruit set, so plant at least two varieties.

name: Wolfberry
description: Wolfberry, also known as Goji berry, is a resilient deciduous shrub prized for its nutritious red berries. It thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, establishing over 1-2 years before producing abundant fruit from late summer into fall.
notes: Ensure good drainage; prune annually in late winter to encourage new fruiting wood.

name: Sorghum
description: Sorghum is a highly drought-tolerant warm-season grain that thrives in hot climates with minimal water. Plant directly after the last frost for harvest of its versatile grain or sweet stalks.
notes: Ensure soil temperatures are consistently above 60°F (15°C) for optimal germination.

name: Milo
description: Milo, also known as grain sorghum, is a drought-tolerant warm-season annual grown primarily for its grain, used extensively as livestock feed. It's a robust crop well-suited for diverse climates and less fertile soils, offering a reliable harvest under challenging conditions.
notes: Ensure good soil drainage and warm soil temperatures for optimal germination and stand establishment.

name: Triticale
description: Triticale is a robust hybrid grain created by crossing wheat and rye, combining the high yield potential of wheat with the hardiness of rye. It is primarily grown as a cool-season annual for forage, feed grain, or as a cover crop, tolerating a wide range of soil conditions and excelling in colder climates.
notes: Benefits from early fall planting for winter varieties and good drainage for optimal growth.

name: Winter Rye
description: Winter rye is a robust cool-season annual grass valued for its excellent winter hardiness and soil-building properties. It is commonly planted in fall to prevent erosion, scavenge nutrients, and add organic matter before spring cash crops.
notes: Establish before first hard frost for best winter survival and spring growth. Terminate before heading for optimal cover crop benefits.

name: Winter Wheat
description: Winter wheat is planted in the fall, establishing roots before going dormant over winter and undergoing vernalization. It resumes vigorous growth in spring for a summer harvest, typically requiring full sun and well-drained soil.
notes: Ensure proper seedbed preparation and adequate nitrogen fertilization in spring for optimal yield and grain quality.

name: Spring Barley
description: Spring barley is a fast-growing, cool-season cereal crop, typically direct sown in early spring after the last hard frost. It requires well-drained soil and full sun, maturing quickly for harvest in mid-summer.
notes: Ensure good soil drainage and monitor for common cereal rusts and blights.

name: Flax
description: Flax thrives in cool, moderate climates with well-drained soils, making it an excellent spring-sown crop. It is harvested for its oil-rich seeds (linseed) or strong fibers, typically maturing within 90-110 days from planting.
notes: Plant early in spring into a firm seedbed to ensure good germination and stand establishment.

name: Canola
description: Canola is a cool-season oilseed crop requiring a full growing season and moderate fertility. It is typically direct-sown in early spring or fall, thriving in well-drained soil with good sun exposure.
notes: Ensure good drainage and even moisture for optimal seed set.

name: Mustard Seed
description: Mustard cover crops grow rapidly, producing significant biomass for weed suppression and soil improvement. As a biofumigant, it releases compounds that can suppress soil-borne pests and diseases upon incorporation.
notes: Incorporate biomass at peak flowering for maximum biofumigant effect.

name: Tillage Radish
description: Tillage radish is an excellent biodrilling cover crop, known for its deep taproot that breaks up compacted soil. It scavenges nutrients, suppresses weeds, and improves soil structure, decaying over winter.
notes: Plant in late summer/early fall for maximum root growth before winterkill and soil conditioning.

name: Crimson Clover
description: Crimson Clover is a beautiful cool-season annual cover crop known for its vibrant red flower spikes and excellent nitrogen-fixing capabilities. It improves soil structure, suppresses weeds, and provides beneficial insect habitat, making it ideal for soil building and crop rotation.
notes: Incorporate into soil at flowering stage for maximum nitrogen contribution and soil organic matter.

name: Hairy Vetch
description: Hairy Vetch is a winter-hardy, nitrogen-fixing legume commonly used as a cover crop. It produces significant biomass, suppresses weeds, and improves soil health by adding organic matter and nitrogen.
notes: Plant in late summer/early fall for best overwintering and spring growth, or in spring for green manure. Terminate before seed set to prevent volunteers.

name: Austrian Winter Pea
description: Austrian Winter Pea is a cold-tolerant legume cover crop known for its nitrogen-fixing capabilities and significant biomass production. It is typically planted in the fall to overwinter and provides erosion control and soil improvement before spring termination.
notes: Plant in fall to overwinter; terminate before flowering for maximum nitrogen retention and to prevent volunteer plants.

name: Sunn Hemp
description: Sunn Hemp is a fast-growing tropical legume used to build soil organic matter and fix nitrogen. It thrives in warm weather and can produce significant biomass in a short period before flowering.
notes: Terminate before flowering to maximize nitrogen fixation and avoid hard seed production.

name: Cowpea Cover
description: Cowpea is a warm-season annual leguminous cover crop known for its rapid growth and excellent nitrogen-fixing capabilities. It thrives in hot weather and poor soils, improving soil structure and suppressing weeds effectively.
notes: Incorporate into soil before seed set for maximum nitrogen benefits and to prevent unwanted reseeding.

name: Annual Ryegrass
description: Annual Ryegrass establishes quickly, providing excellent erosion control and weed suppression. It rapidly produces significant biomass, improving soil structure and organic matter content.
notes: Best planted in fall or early spring; terminates easily before setting seed.

name: Phacelia Cover
description: Phacelia is a fast-growing, cool-season annual cover crop known for its beautiful purple blooms. It excels at attracting a wide range of beneficial insects and pollinators, enhancing garden biodiversity.
notes: Broadcast seed thickly for best weed suppression and maximum pollinator attraction.

name: Berseem Clover
description: Berseem Clover is a fast-growing, multi-cut cool-season annual that excels as a nitrogen-fixing cover crop and high-quality forage. It thrives in moderate climates, improving soil health and providing successive harvests of leafy biomass.
notes: Ensure good seed-to-soil contact for optimal germination and multiple cuts before flowering.

name: Field Peas
description: Field peas are a cool-season annual legume valued for their protein-rich forage and soil-building properties. Plant in early spring or late summer for quick biomass production and nitrogen fixation.
notes: Ensure good drainage and sow densely for weed suppression and optimal forage yield.

name: Fababean
description: Fababeans are a cool-season legume known for improving soil health and providing a protein-rich grain crop. They thrive in well-drained soil and require consistent moisture, especially during pod development.
notes: Plant early in spring to avoid heat stress, or late summer for an overwintering crop in mild climates.

name: Soybean
description: Soybeans are warm-season legumes grown for their edible pods (edamame) or mature dry beans. They require well-drained soil and full sun, and are typically direct-seeded after the last frost.
notes: Ensure soil temperatures are consistently above 60°F (15°C) for optimal germination and growth.

name: Lupin
description: Lupin is a cool-season grain legume known for its ability to fix nitrogen in the soil. It prefers well-drained, slightly acidic to neutral soils and can tolerate light frost, making it suitable for early spring planting.
notes: Ensure good drainage to prevent root diseases; excellent as a cover crop or cash crop.

name: Safflower
description: Safflower thrives in warm, dry conditions and is relatively drought-tolerant once established. It is cultivated for its oil-rich seeds, used in cooking and birdseed, and its colorful flowers.
notes: Spiny varieties offer pest protection but make manual harvest more challenging.

name: Ethiopian Kale
description: Ethiopian Kale, also known as Gomen, is a robust, heat-tolerant Brassica celebrated for its large, nutritious leaves. It thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, offering a continuous harvest over a long season.
notes: This African Brassica tolerates warmer temperatures better than many other kales, making it suitable for extended growing seasons but still prefers cool for optimal flavor.

name: Siberian Kale
description: Siberian Kale is an ultra-hardy, blue-green kale type known for its excellent cold tolerance and tender leaves. It thrives in cool weather, producing abundant harvests through light frosts and even snow.
notes: Harvest outer leaves regularly to encourage continuous production and maintain tenderness.

name: Portuguese Kale
description: Portuguese Kale thrives in cool weather and can tolerate light frosts, which often sweetens its flavor. Harvest outer leaves regularly to encourage continuous production throughout the growing season.
notes: Harvest outer leaves first to extend the harvest window and promote new growth.

name: Red Russian Kale
description: Red Russian Kale is a frilly, purple-stemmed tender kale variety known for its sweet, mild flavor. It thrives in cool weather and can tolerate light frost, which often enhances its taste.
notes: Harvest outer leaves regularly to encourage continuous production and maintain tenderness.

name: Lacinato Kale
description: Lacinato Kale, also known as Dinosaur or Tuscan kale, is a cool-season brassica valued for its dark, puckered leaves and robust flavor. It thrives in cool weather and can be harvested repeatedly by taking outer leaves, providing a continuous yield.
notes: Ensure consistent moisture for tender leaves and protect from common brassica pests like flea beetles.

name: Savoy Cabbage
description: Savoy Cabbage is a crinkle-leaved headed brassica known for its mild flavor and tender texture. It thrives in cool weather, requiring consistent moisture and fertile soil to develop firm, flavorful heads.
notes: Ensure even moisture for best head development and prevent splitting.

name: Red Cabbage
description: Red Cabbage is a cool-season crop that thrives in rich, well-drained soil with consistent moisture. It requires ample space and protection from common Brassica pests like cabbage worms for optimal head development.
notes: Ensure consistent watering, especially during head formation, to prevent splitting.

name: Bok Choy
description: Bok Choy is a fast-growing, white-stemmed Asian brassica that prefers cool weather. It's best harvested when young and tender, before it bolts in heat.
notes: Ensure consistent moisture to prevent bolting and bitterness.

name: Tyfon
description: Tyfon is a fast-growing Brassica hybrid, prized for its vigorous leafy growth and deep roots. It serves as an excellent cool-season cover crop, offering high-quality forage for livestock and improving soil health.
notes: Provides excellent grazing potential and weed suppression; can be grazed multiple times before incorporation.

name: Forage Rape
description: Forage rape is a fast-growing, cool-season brassica primarily used as a cover crop or for livestock grazing. It offers excellent weed suppression, soil improvement, and can provide high-quality forage into late fall.
notes: Ensure good soil moisture during establishment for best biomass production.

name: Swede
description: Swede is a hardy cool-season root vegetable, known for its dense yellow flesh and sweet flavor. It thrives in well-drained soil and can tolerate light frosts, which often enhance its taste.
notes: Ensure consistent moisture for best root development and to prevent cracking.

name: Celtuce
description: Celtuce, also known as stem lettuce, is a cool-season crop prized for its crisp stem and edible leaves. It thrives in mild temperatures and will bolt in hot weather, so time plantings for spring or fall.
notes: Harvest the stem when it is thick and tender, typically before a significant heat wave or bolting occurs.

name: Shungiku
description: Shungiku, or edible chrysanthemum, is a fast-growing cool-season herb known for its unique tangy flavor. Direct sow seeds in early spring or late summer for quick harvests, preferring fertile, well-draining soil and consistent moisture.
notes: Harvest young leaves frequently to encourage new growth and prevent bolting in warmer weather.

name: Japanese Eggplant
description: Japanese eggplant thrives in warm soil and full sun, requiring consistent moisture and nutrients for optimal growth. Plants produce slender, elongated fruits over a long harvest season until frost.
notes: Stake plants to support heavy fruit loads and encourage upright growth, ensuring good air circulation.

name: Holy Basil
description: Holy Basil thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, preferring warm temperatures and succumbing to frost. Pinch back flower buds regularly to encourage bushy growth and maximize leaf production.
notes: Regular pinching of flowers and top leaves promotes continuous harvest and prevents woody stems.

name: Cinnamon Basil
description: Cinnamon Basil is a heat-loving annual herb that adds a unique spiced aroma to dishes. It prefers full sun, rich well-drained soil, and consistent moisture, requiring protection from any frost.
notes: Pinch regularly above a leaf node to encourage branching and extend the harvest window.

name: Lime Basil
description: Lime Basil thrives in warm, sunny conditions and well-drained soil, producing compact plants with a bright, citrusy aroma. Regular harvesting by pinching back the top leaves will encourage bushier growth and delay flowering.
notes: Pinch flowers regularly to extend harvest and maintain leaf quality.

name: African Blue Basil
description: African Blue Basil is a vigorous, sterile hybrid perennial basil known for its beautiful purple flowers and camphoraceous scent. It thrives in warm weather, requires consistent moisture, and benefits from regular harvesting to encourage bushiness and continuous leaf production.
notes: Pinch regularly to encourage branching and prevent woody stems. Excellent pollinator magnet.

name: Pineapple Sage
description: Pineapple Sage is a fragrant perennial herb known for its bright red flowers and leaves with a distinct pineapple scent. It thrives in full sun to partial shade and well-drained soil, making it a vibrant addition to herb gardens and attracting pollinators.
notes: Harvest leaves and flowers regularly to encourage bushier growth and prolong the season.

name: Clary Sage
description: Clary Sage is a biennial herb known for its large leaves and tall flower spikes, often grown for its medicinal and aromatic properties. It thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, forming a rosette in its first year and blooming profusely in its second.
notes: Ensure good air circulation and deadhead spent flowers to encourage vigor or allow to self-seed for future crops.

name: Black Sage
description: Black Sage is a drought-tolerant native California perennial known for its aromatic leaves and flowers. It thrives in full sun and well-draining soil, making it suitable for low-water landscapes and medicinal herb production.
notes: Plant in full sun with excellent drainage; minimal water needed once established.

name: Roman Chamomile
description: Roman Chamomile is a low-growing perennial herb valued for its apple-scented flowers and ground cover capabilities. Harvest abundant daisy-like blooms regularly to encourage continuous flowering throughout the growing season.
notes: Provide good drainage and full sun for best flowering; tolerates light foot traffic once established.

name: Agastache
description: Agastache, or Hummingbird Mint, is a beautiful perennial herb known for its fragrant leaves and spikes of flowers that attract pollinators. It thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, offering continuous harvests of edible leaves and flowers throughout the warm season.
notes: Deadhead regularly to encourage continuous blooming and a bushier plant.

name: Bergamot
description: Bee Balm thrives in full sun to partial shade with moist, well-drained soil, and good air circulation to prevent powdery mildew. As a perennial, it benefits from division every few years to maintain vigor and prevent over-crowding.
notes: Pinch back young plants to encourage bushiness and more flower production.

name: Blue Vervain
description: Blue Vervain is a hardy perennial herb thriving in full sun to partial shade and moist, well-drained soils. Known as a valuable pollinator plant, it produces delicate purple flowers in summer and is harvested for its leaves and flowering tops.
notes: Attracts a wide range of pollinators including bees and butterflies, and can naturalize in favorable conditions.

name: Licorice Root
description: Glycyrrhiza glabra, commonly known as Licorice Root, is a perennial herb cultivated for its sweet, medicinal roots. It thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, requiring 2-4 years to mature before the roots are ready for harvest.
notes: Requires patience; roots typically harvested after 3-4 years of growth in fall.

name: Spilanthes
description: Spilanthes thrives in warm weather and full sun, preferring well-drained soil. Begin harvesting its numbing flowers and leaves once plants are established, continuing throughout the season until frost.
notes: Pinch back regularly to encourage bushier growth and more flower production.

name: Wood Sorrel
description: Wood sorrel is a low-growing perennial herb known for its clover-like leaves and tart, lemony flavor, often used in salads, as a garnish, or in teas. It thrives in cool, moist, shaded environments and can spread readily, forming an attractive ground cover.
notes: Harvest outer leaves regularly to encourage continuous production; tolerates light shade and moist soil. Can also be propagated by division.

name: Wood Betony
description: A hardy perennial woodland medicinal herb, Wood Betony thrives in partial shade and moist, well-drained soil. It produces attractive spikes of purple flowers and can be harvested for its leaves and flowers from late spring to autumn.
notes: Ensure consistent moisture, especially during establishment, and consider partial shade for best growth.

name: Echinacea Purpurea
description: Echinacea Purpurea, or purple coneflower, is a hardy perennial herb known for its immune-boosting properties, with both flowers, leaves, and roots used medicinally. It thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, providing continuous harvests of beautiful purple blooms and foliage through the warm season.
notes: Ensure good drainage; benefits from occasional side-dressing with compost once established.

name: Sweet Cicely
description: Sweet Cicely is a hardy perennial herb that thrives in cool, moist, and partially shaded conditions. It establishes slowly from seed but provides anise-flavored leaves for early spring to summer harvest.
notes: Requires patience for establishment; consider transplanting divisions for quicker harvest.

name: Ground Elder
description: Ground elder is a vigorous perennial herb known for its edible young leaves, which have a mild, slightly peppery taste. It spreads aggressively via rhizomes, making it important to plant in contained areas or where its spread is desired.
notes: Manage spread carefully as it can become invasive; regular harvesting helps keep it in check and encourages tender new growth.

name: Nettle
description: Nettle is a nutrient-rich perennial herb best harvested in early spring when leaves are young and tender. It prefers moist, fertile soil and partial shade, spreading easily once established.
notes: Wear gloves when harvesting to avoid stings; blanching removes the sting.

name: Plantain Herb
description: Plantain is a resilient perennial herb that thrives in various conditions, providing nutrient-rich leaves for culinary and medicinal uses. It is best grown from direct-sown seeds in spring or fall, tolerating light foot traffic and poor soil.
notes: Harvest outer leaves regularly to encourage new growth and maintain plant vigor.

name: Yarrow
description: Yarrow is a hardy perennial flower that thrives in full sun and well-drained soil, making it excellent for low-maintenance cut flower production. It is drought-tolerant once established and produces abundant feathery white blooms that attract pollinators.
notes: Cut stems when flowers are fully open for best vase life and to encourage further blooming.

name: Feverfew
description: Feverfew is a hardy perennial herb known for its small, daisy-like flowers and medicinal properties, thriving in full sun to partial shade. It prefers well-drained soil and establishes well from transplants for a season-long harvest of leaves and flowers.
notes: Deadhead regularly to prolong blooming and manage self-seeding, or allow to self-sow for naturalized areas.

name: St John's Wort
description: St. John's Wort thrives in full sun to partial shade and well-drained soil, tolerating a range of conditions once established. Harvest flowering tops when the plant is in full bloom for medicinal use, often done annually from established perennial plants.
notes: Ensure good drainage and ample sunlight for robust growth and optimal flowering.

name: Rhodiola
description: Rhodiola is a hardy perennial herb best suited for cool climates, taking 3-5 years to mature its valuable medicinal roots. It prefers well-drained soil and can be started from seed or transplant, thriving even with a short growing season.
notes: Ensure excellent drainage, especially during winter, to prevent root rot.

name: Maca
description: Maca is an Andean crucifer grown for its nutrient-dense taproot, thriving in cool, high-altitude environments. It requires a long growing season to fully develop its medicinal and culinary roots, typically planted in spring for a fall or winter harvest.
notes: Ensure good drainage and consistent moisture, especially during root development.

name: Arracacha
description: Arracacha thrives in cool, humid, high-altitude tropical conditions with fertile, well-drained soil. It is primarily propagated by planting small rootlet divisions and requires a long, frost-free growing season to mature its starchy roots.
notes: Ensure consistent moisture and protect from frost. Harvest when leaves begin to yellow.

name: Tiger Nut
description: Tiger nuts (chufa sedge) produce sweet, nutty tubers underground. They thrive in warm weather and well-drained soil, forming numerous small, edible rhizomes.
notes: Ensure consistent moisture during tuber development but allow soil to dry for harvest.

name: Lotus Root
description: Lotus root thrives in shallow, muddy ponds or large containers, requiring warm temperatures and abundant sunlight for optimal growth. Plant rhizomes in spring for a fall and winter harvest of starchy, crunchy tubers.
notes: Requires consistent warm temperatures and adequate water depth; ideal for ponds or large aquatic containers.

name: Water Chestnut
description: Water chestnuts require a long, warm growing season in flooded conditions, typically in shallow ponds or large containers. Harvest corms from the muck after the foliage dies back in late fall, then wash thoroughly.
notes: Ensure consistent water levels (4-6 inches deep) and warm temperatures for optimal corm development.

name: Wasabi
description: Requires consistent cool temperatures, high humidity, and partial to full shade. Prefers well-drained, constantly moist soil and takes 18-24 months to mature.
notes: Ensure consistent water flow or high humidity; avoid direct sunlight and standing water.

name: Wakame
description: Wakame (Undaria pinnatifida) is a brown seaweed cultivated in cool, clear ocean waters, often attached to submerged ropes or structures. It thrives in nutrient-rich marine environments and is harvested when fronds reach optimal size and tenderness.
notes: Requires specific marine aquaculture setup and cool water temperatures.

name: Agretti
description: Agretti, or Salsola soda, is a fast-growing cool-season annual known for its succulent, slightly salty leaves. Direct seed in early spring or fall, harvesting young shoots repeatedly before they become fibrous or bolt in warm weather.
notes: Ensure consistent moisture for tender growth and harvest frequently to encourage new shoots.

name: Samphire
description: Samphire thrives in well-drained, sandy soils, preferring full sun and coastal conditions. It benefits from light salinity in its irrigation water, enhancing its unique flavor and crunch.
notes: Consider adding a small amount of sea salt to irrigation water for optimal flavor development.

name: Sea Purslane
description: Sea Purslane is a resilient, salt-tolerant succulent that thrives in warm coastal conditions, perfect for specialty greens. It can be direct sown or propagated from cuttings, requiring minimal care once established and tolerating poor soils.
notes: Tolerates saline conditions and thrives with good drainage.

name: Glasswort
description: Glasswort (Salicornia) is a succulent, salty plant typically found in coastal areas, prized for its crunchy texture and briny flavor. It thrives in saline soils or with salty water irrigation, making it suitable for unique growing environments.
notes: Requires saline conditions for optimal growth and flavor; can be irrigated with brackish water.

name: Skirret
description: Skirret is a perennial root vegetable grown for its sweet, multi-rooted clusters, often described as a cross between a parsnip and a potato. It thrives in cool weather and moist soil, requiring a long growing season for optimal root development.
notes: Harvest roots after the first hard frost for improved sweetness, or leave them in the ground until needed.

name: Good King Henry
description: A hardy, low-maintenance perennial green, Good King Henry provides spinach-like leaves and asparagus-like flower shoots in spring. It thrives in cool weather and partial shade, making it a valuable addition to perennial vegetable beds.
notes: Harvest young leaves regularly to encourage new growth and prevent bolting. Can spread vigorously once established.

name: Nine Star Broccoli
description: Nine Star Broccoli is a perennial Brassica producing multiple small white heads over many years, primarily in spring. It thrives in cooler weather, requiring adequate spacing for its bushy growth and an established period before its main harvest season.
notes: Harvest individual florets before they flower to encourage continuous production and prevent bolting.

name: Perennial Kale
description: Perennial kale varieties like Tronchuda or Tree Kale offer continuous harvests over multiple years, providing a reliable source of greens. These hardy Brassicas grow large and can withstand light frosts, making them a productive addition to perennial beds.
notes: Harvest outer leaves regularly to encourage new growth and maintain plant vigor.

name: Walking Onion
description: A unique perennial Allium that produces small edible bulblets at the top of its stalks, which fall over and root to 'walk' across the garden. It provides a continuous supply of green onions, small bulbs, and savory bulblets with minimal effort.
notes: Extremely hardy and low-maintenance, walking onions are an excellent choice for a permanent bed, providing early spring greens and easy propagation via bulblets.

name: Welsh Onion
description: Welsh onions are hardy perennial bunching onions that never form true bulbs, providing continuous harvests of green stalks. They are easy to grow and propagate, thriving in cool weather and tolerating frost.
notes: Harvest by cutting outer leaves or entire clumps, allowing them to regrow for multiple harvests.

name: Japanese Bunching Onion
description: Japanese Bunching Onions are slender, white-stemmed perennial onions that can be harvested by cutting the green tops repeatedly. They are relatively low-maintenance, thriving in cool weather and providing a continuous supply of mild onion flavor.
notes: Cut outer leaves to encourage continuous regrowth; established plants can overwinter in many climates.

name: Potato Onion
description: Potato onions are an easy-to-grow multiplier onion, producing clusters of small-to-medium bulbs from a single planted set. Plant sets in early spring or fall for a harvest in early to mid-summer, allowing tops to fall over before lifting.
notes: Often planted in fall for an earlier, larger spring harvest, requiring minimal maintenance.

name: Rocambole Garlic
description: Rocambole is a hardneck garlic known for its rich, complex, true garlic flavor, ideal for culinary use. It produces large bulbs with easy-to-peel cloves and edible scapes in late spring.
notes: Ensure adequate vernalization by planting in fall; harvest scapes to encourage larger bulbs.

name: Elephant Garlic
description: Elephant garlic is a large, mild-flavored member of the Allium family, more closely related to a leek than true garlic. It is planted in fall, overwinters, and produces impressive large bulbs with several cloves by late spring to early summer.
notes: Ensure good drainage and consistent moisture, especially during bulb development.

name: Garlic Chives
description: Garlic Chives are a hardy perennial herb that provides a continuous harvest of flat, garlic-flavored leaves. They thrive in full sun to partial shade and well-drained soil, tolerating a range of conditions.
notes: Cut back regularly to encourage new growth and prevent flowering; flowers are also edible.

name: Shallot
description: Shallots are mild, multiplying onions that form clumps of bulbs. Plant in early spring for a summer harvest, ensuring good drainage and consistent moisture.
notes: Harvest when tops begin to yellow and fall over, then cure in a warm, dry place for storage.

name: Pickling Onion
description: Pickling onions are small, tender silverskin onions harvested young for culinary use, especially pickling. They prefer fertile, well-drained soil and full sun, thriving in cooler spring or fall conditions.
notes: Thin aggressively for consistent sizing and to prevent overcrowding, ensuring good air circulation.

name: Torpedo Onion
description: Torpedo onions are long, red, sweet Italian onions best grown in full sun and well-draining, fertile soil. They require consistent moisture throughout the growing season for optimal bulb development and sweetness.
notes: Ensure adequate nitrogen early in the season, reducing it as bulbs begin to form.

name: Sweet Onion
description: Sweet onions thrive in rich, well-drained soil with consistent moisture, forming large, mild bulbs. Plant transplants in early spring after the last frost for harvest when tops begin to fall over.
notes: Ensure consistent moisture during bulb formation for optimal sweetness and size, and do not over-fertilize with nitrogen late in the season.
